name: azure-mgmt-apimanagement-py
description: This skill provides a comprehensive guide to using the Azure API Management SDK for Python. It helps developers create, manage, and publish APIs through Azure API Management service using Python code.
capabilities: Generate Python code for Azure API Management operations
Create and configure API Management instances
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation and guidance for the Azure API Management SDK for Python. The static analyzer scanned 0 files and detected 0 potential security issues. The skill provides educational content about Azure API Management without any executable code that could pose security risks.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers integrate and manage Azure API Management services using Python, enabling automated API deployment and management workflows.
userTitle: Manage Azure APIs with Python SDK
limitations: Does not provide live Azure API Management credentials
Cannot execute actual API calls without user configuration
Requires valid Azure subscription for real-world usage
Generated code templates require Azure SDK installation

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Automating API Deployments
useCases.description: Automate the creation and configuration of API Management instances as part of CI/CD pipelines using Python scripts.

useCases.title: Backend Developer Building API Gateways
useCases.description: Use the SDK to programmatically create APIs, configure rate limiting, and set up authentication for backend services.

useCases.targetUser: Platform engineers responsible for API infrastructure
useCases.title: Platform Engineer Managing API Infrastructure
useCases.description: Manage multiple API Management instances, products, and subscriptions across different environments.

promptTemplates.title: Create a basic API in Azure API Management
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a new API in Azure API Management using the Python SDK. Include code for setting up the API, adding operations, and configuring a backend service.

promptTemplates.title: Configure API authentication policies
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure JWT validation and OAuth2 authentication policies for an API in Azure API Management using Python?

promptTemplates.title: Import OpenAPI specification
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Python code to import an OpenAPI 3.0 specification into Azure API Management and generate documentation.

promptTemplates.title: Set up rate limiting and quotas
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to configure rate limiting policies and product subscription quotas using the Azure API Management Python SDK.

bestPractices: Use managed identities for authentication instead of storing credentials in code
Organize APIs by products and use subscription keys for access control
Apply versioning to APIs from the start to enable smooth updates
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode subscription keys or secrets in source code
Avoid configuring policies directly in production without testing
Do not expose backend services directly without API Management layer

faq.question: What is Azure API Management?
faq.answer: Azure API Management is a cloud service that helps organizations publish, manage, secure, and analyze APIs. It provides a gateway for API traffic, caching, authentication, and logging.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: You do not need an Azure subscription to learn from this skill. However, to execute the generated code, you will need an active Azure subscription.

faq.question: What Python version is supported?
faq.answer: The Azure API Management SDK for Python supports Python 3.7 and later versions.

faq.question: Can this skill help with API versioning?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes guidance on setting up and managing API versions using Azure API Management.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure?
faq.answer: You can use Azure Active Directory, managed identities, or service principal credentials. The recommended approach is using DefaultAzureCredential for local development.

faq.question: Can I import existing OpenAPI specifications?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure API Management supports importing OpenAPI 2.0 and 3.0 specifications, and this skill covers how to do it programmatically.
